욥기 34:2.content: “Hear my words, you wise men. Give ear to me, you who have knowledge.

욥기 34:3.content: For the ear tries words, as the palate tastes food.

욥기 34:4.content: Let us choose for us that which is right. Let us know among ourselves what is good.

욥기 34:5.content: For Job has said, ‘I am righteous, God has taken away my right:

욥기 34:6.content: Notwithstanding my right I am considered a liar. My wound is incurable, though I am without disobedience.’

욥기 34:7.content: What man is like Job, who drinks scorn like water,

욥기 34:8.content: Who goes in company with the workers of iniquity, and walks with wicked men?

욥기 34:9.content: For he has said, ‘It profits a man nothing that he should delight himself with God.’

욥기 34:10.content: “Therefore listen to me, you men of understanding: far be it from God, that he should do wickedness, from the Almighty, that he should commit iniquity.

욥기 34:11.content: For the work of a man he will render to him, and cause every man to find according to his ways.

욥기 34:12.content: Yes surely, God will not do wickedly, neither will the Almighty pervert justice.

욥기 34:13.content: Who put him in charge of the earth? Or who has appointed him over the whole world?

욥기 34:14.content: If he set his heart on himself, if he gathered to himself his spirit and his breath,

욥기 34:15.content: all flesh would perish together, and man would turn again to dust.

욥기 34:16.content: “If now you have understanding, hear this. Listen to the voice of my words.

욥기 34:17.content: Should even one who hates justice govern? Will you condemn him who is righteous and mighty?—

욥기 34:18.content: Who says to a king, ‘Vile!’ or to nobles, ‘Wicked!’?

욥기 34:19.content: He doesn’t respect the persons of princes, nor respect the rich more than the poor; for they all are the work of his hands.

욥기 34:20.content: In a moment they die, even at midnight. The people are shaken and pass away. The mighty are taken away without a hand.

욥기 34:21.content: “For his eyes are on the ways of a man. He sees all his goings.

욥기 34:22.content: There is no darkness, nor thick gloom, where the workers of iniquity may hide themselves.

욥기 34:23.content: For he doesn’t need to consider a man further, that he should go before God in judgment.

욥기 34:24.content: He breaks mighty men in pieces in ways past finding out, and sets others in their place.

욥기 34:25.content: Therefore he takes knowledge of their works. He overturns them in the night, so that they are destroyed.

욥기 34:26.content: He strikes them as wicked men in the open sight of others;

욥기 34:27.content: because they turned away from following him, and wouldn’t pay attention to any of his ways,

욥기 34:28.content: so that they caused the cry of the poor to come to him. He heard the cry of the afflicted.

욥기 34:29.content: When he gives quietness, who then can condemn? When he hides his face, who then can see him? He is over a nation or a man alike,

욥기 34:30.content: that the godless man may not reign, that there be no one to ensnare the people.

욥기 34:31.content: “For has any said to God, ‘I am guilty, but I will not offend any more.

욥기 34:32.content: Teach me that which I don’t see. If I have done iniquity, I will do it no more’?

욥기 34:33.content: Shall his recompense be as you desire, that you refuse it? For you must choose, and not I. Therefore speak what you know.

욥기 34:34.content: Men of understanding will tell me, yes, every wise man who hears me:

욥기 34:35.content: ‘Job speaks without knowledge. His words are without wisdom.’

욥기 34:36.content: I wish that Job were tried to the end, because of his answering like wicked men.

욥기 34:37.content: For he adds rebellion to his sin. He claps his hands among us, and multiplies his words against God.”
